rulings.Case Title: RODRICK BRECKLER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION TO CONTINUE IS GRANTED AND THE MOTION IS SET TO BE HEARD ON NOVEMBER 13, 2008. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE BRECKLER AND COHEN DECLARATIONS ARE SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION AND RELIANCE ON HEARSAY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARY PAPPAS VS. EUGENE TELEGIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Written Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 3, PLAINTIFF MARY PAPPAS Motion To Enforce Written Settlement Agreement, HEARING REQUIRED FOR THE PARTIES TO PROVIDE AUTHORITY AS TO HOW LONG MEDICARE HAS TO MAKE A CLAIM. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES LOUIE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANT NORTHROP GRUMMAN SHIP SYSTEMS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO VS. D.N. SECURITY SERVICES, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs;Application
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 5, PLAINTIFF MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO'S Notice Of Hearing For Application For Attorney's Fees And Costs, HEARING REQUIRED. THE PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE CASE AUTHORITY THAT SUPPORTS OR DOES NOT SUPPORT AN AWARD OF FEES FOR WORK DONE ON BEHALF OF A CLASS BUT THE CASE IS SETTLED ONLY ON BEHALF OF THE NAMED PLAINTIFF. THE PARTIES ARE TO EXPLAIN THE STATUS OF THE CLAIMS OF THE OTHER CLASS MEMBERS. WAS CLASS CERTIFICATION EVER SOUGHT? =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SHERIDAN CONCEPTS & CONSTRUCTION VS. HUGH MEAKIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Remove Or Reduce Mechanic'S Lien
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 6, DEFENDANTS HUGH MEAKIN, MELBA MEAKIN, SARAH MEAKIN, AND PHILLIP MEAKIN'S Motion For An Order To Remove Or Reduce Mechanic's Lien. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE PROCEDURE IS AUTHORIZED BY HOWARD S. WRIGHT CONSTRUCTION CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 106 CAL. APP. 4TH 314, 318. THE MEAKIN DECLARATION ASSERTS THAT SHERIDAN HAD NO CONTRACT WITH THE OWNERS. THE EVIDENCE OFFERED IN THE CAMPBELL DECLARATION IS NOT ADMISSIBLE FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION, AUTHENTICATION AND HEARSAY. AS A CONSEQUENCE PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO GIVE DEFENDANTS 20 DAY NOTICE AND THAT WAS NOT DONE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: REGINALD HINDLEY VS. ZACKS UTRECHT & LEADBETTER,A PROFESSIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Vacating And Setting Aside Dismissal And Related Orders
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 8, PLAINTIFF REGINALD HINDLEY'S Motion For Order To Vacating And Setting Aside Dismissal And Related Orders IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE CASE WAS RESOLVED AFTER PLAINTIFF'S CONVERSATION WITH MR. EMILSON. THE DISMISSAL IS VACATED AS IS THE ORDER GRANTING THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CDF FIREFIGHTERS, A LABOR ORGANIZATION ON BEHALF et al VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is off calendar in light of the ruling on the demurrer. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CDF FIREFIGHTERS, A LABOR ORGANIZATION ON BEHALF et al VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend because the statute of limitations, Government Code section 19815.8 has run. Government Code section 20164(2) states that it is not applicable in cases where the system owes money to a member or beneficiary. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CDF FIREFIGHTERS, A LABOR ORGANIZATION ON BEHALF et al VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 3, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. The issue presented - the parties' different interpretations of a statute - is not a ground for demurrer where all inferences must be read in favor of plaintiff. =(302/PJM/JW)
